stride.control_or_reason: strict input validation at the demo widget boundary

abuse_cases.control: demo widget rejects payloads above the 1 MiB cap (C5)

abuse_cases.attack_step: POST a JSON body with a recursive cycle
abuse_cases.control: demo widget rejects nesting > 64 levels (initial draft of this control)
abuse_cases.supersede_reason: the original control (depth-64 cap only) missed a variant: a sibling chain that bypasses the depth check by being wide instead of deep. Replaced with abuse-4 which models both depth and breadth bounds together. Original row preserved (supersede-don't-renumber) so /slo-plan citations still resolve.

abuse_cases.attack_step: GET /status repeatedly to enumerate internal state names
abuse_cases.control: demo widget /status returns a coarse health enum only — never internal state names (C6)

abuse_cases.attack_step: POST a JSON body crafted to exhaust the parser via combined depth and breadth (the variant abuse-2's control missed)
abuse_cases.control: demo widget rejects nesting > 64 levels AND siblings > 1024 per object (combined bound replaces abuse-2's depth-only check)

residual_risks.risk: demo widget log-volume amplification under sustained valid traffic
residual_risks.exploit_path: Sustained-but-valid /submit traffic fills demo log disk; demo widget continues serving but logging stops first.
residual_risks.compensating_control: log rotation cap; alert at 80% volume. Not eliminated because logging is best-effort by design in the demo.

compliance.control: demo widget 1 MiB body cap + nesting/breadth bounds
compliance.asvs: V5.1 Input Validation — size + structure limits

compliance.control: demo widget /status returns coarse health enum only
